description_long: This object is a classic fire hydrant, predominantly cylindrical with a domed top and a circular base featuring bolt details. It has two large, rounded side outlets and a central vertical body, all rendered in a textured reddish-brown color typical of cast iron hydrants. The top of the hydrant features a small, dark cap or operating nut, while the outlets are capped and protrude symmetrically from the main body. The surface appears slightly rough, suggesting a cast metal finish, and the base is ringed with evenly spaced bolt heads. There are no visible chains, manufacturer markings, or additional attachments. The overall form is robust and utilitarian, designed for durability and easy access in emergency situations.
description: A robust, reddish-brown fire hydrant with two large side outlets, a domed top, and a circular base with bolt details. The surface is slightly rough, resembling cast iron.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large side outlets and the domed top with a small central cap.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one large outlet protruding from the cylindrical body, with the other outlet hidden.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying both side outlets and the domed top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the other large outlet and the cylindrical body.
